ACCESS CONTROL & INIT — - **ERC4337FactoryWrapper::deploy** — Restricts deployment to `senderCreator` only — If `senderCreator` is compromised or set incorrectly, all wallet deployments are blocked or hijacked - **Estimator::_isValidImage** — Always returns `true` overriding validation — Any signature/imageHash accepted; attacker can execute arbitrary payloads - **Hooks::addHook** — Hook can overwrite existing selector if check fails — Race condition or check bypass allows hook replacement, enabling malicious delegatecall target - **Hooks::removeHook** — No validation that caller owns hook — Could be bypassed if `_readHook` returns stale data - **Implementation::updateImplementation** — No validation of `_implementation` address — Can set to zero or malicious address, bricking or hijacking wallet - **Recovery::queuePayload** — No rate limiting or array-length caps on `queuedPayloadHashes` — Griefing via unbounded array growth (DoS) - **SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature** — External caller is `msg.sender` (wallet) but no authentication beyond that — If wallet proxy is misconfigured, unauthorized calls possible

ARITHMETIC — - **SessionSig::recoverConfiguration** — `permissionsCount` incremented unchecked, could exceed array length if `maxPermissionsSize` guess is wrong — Out-of-bounds write risk, memory corruption - **SessionSig::_decodePermissions** — Reads `uint8 length`, but no check that encoded data has enough bytes — Underflow/out-of-bounds read if malformed - **LibBytes (implicit via SessionSig)** — Multiple `readUintX` / `readBytes32` assume sufficient data, but no boundary checks in some paths — Truncation or revert on short input - **PermissionValidator::validatePermission** — `uint256 value256 = uint256(value)` cast from `bytes32` — Potential data loss if `mask` is incorrect - **SessionSig::recoverSignature** — Pointer arithmetic `pointer += ...` unchecked in several places — Could wrap or exceed `encodedSignature.length` causing OOB - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `nonceSize` derived from bitfield `(globalFlag >> 1) & 0x07` — If `nonceSize` is 0, nonce is zero; no validation that this is intended - **LibBytes::readUintX** — `_sizeBytes` controls how many bytes to read, but if `_sizeBytes > 32`, no revert — Could read garbage or revert unpredictably - **SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature** — `sessionUsageLimits` array resized via assembly `mstore(actualSessionUsageLimits, actualSize)` — Off-by-one or incorrect size could cause memory overlap - **ExplicitSessionManager::_validateLimitUsageIncrement** — `uint256 totalLimitsLength` summed without overflow check — Very large arrays could wrap, leading to incorrect array allocation - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `numCalls` read as uint8 or uint16, but no check against maximum reasonable limit — Could allocate huge `Call[]` array, DoS - **Nonce::_consumeNonce** — `unchecked { uint256 newNonce = _nonce + 1 }` — Nonce wraps at `2^256-1`, allowing replay after wrap - **SessionSig::recoverConfiguration** — `blacklistCount` read from nibble or uint16, but no cap — Could allocate huge `address[]`, DoS

TOKEN ACCOUNTING — - **Calls::_execute** — No checks on return values of external calls (via `LibOptim.call`) — Fee-on-transfer or revert-on-zero-approval tokens could fail silently - **ERC4337v07::validateUserOp** — `missingAccountFunds` sent via `depositTo` without checking wallet balance — Could revert if wallet has insufficient balance, blocking UserOp - **Guest::_dispatchGuest** — Native token sent via `call.value` without checking balance — Could fail silently or revert, leaving inconsistent state - **Hooks::receive** — Accepts ETH without event or tracking — Griefing or lost funds if no withdraw mechanism - **Calls::_execute** — `call.value` transferred without verifying `address(this).balance >= call.value` — Could revert if balance insufficient - **SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature** — `sessionUsageLimits[i].totalValueUsed` incremented without checking wallet balance — Limit enforcement off-chain only, on-chain state could diverge

ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - **SessionSig::recoverSignature** — `attestationIndex` read from `uint8`, checked `< attestationList.length`, but `attestationList` built from `attestationCount` which is unbounded — Large `attestationCount` could cause OOB - **SessionSig::recoverConfiguration** — `blacklistCount` read from nibble (0-14) or uint16, but no validation against reasonable max — DoS via huge array - **SessionSig::_decodePermissions** — `length` read as uint8, but no validation that encoded data has `length * MIN_ENCODED_PERMISSION_SIZE` bytes — OOB read - **Simulator::simulate** — `_calls` array length not validated — Could run out of gas or exceed block limit - **Estimator::estimate** — `decoded.calls` length not validated — DoS via large payload - **Recovery::queuePayload** — `queuedPayloadHashes[_wallet][_signer]` unbounded array — DoS via unlimited queuing - **ImplicitSessionManager::_isAddressBlacklisted** — Binary search assumes sorted array, but only one check in `recoverConfiguration` — If blacklist is unsorted, blacklist check fails - **PermissionValidator::validatePermission** — `permission.rules.length` unbounded — DoS via large rule array - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `numCalls` up to uint16 (65535) — Allocating 65k `Call[]` could exhaust memory or gas - **SessionSig::recoverConfiguration** — `maxPermissionsSize = encoded.length / MIN_ENCODED_PERMISSION_SIZE` — Could allocate massive array if `encoded.length` is large, DoS - **SessionSig::recoverSignature** — `callsCount = payload.calls.length` used to size `sig.callSignatures` — No check that `callsCount` matches encoded signature length, could mismatch - **LibPermission::readPermission** — `rulesLength` read as uint8, but no check that `encoded[pointer:]` has enough data — OOB read

EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY — - **Calls::_execute** — Calls external contracts via `LibOptim.call` and `delegatecall` without reentrancy guard on entire function — CEI violated if `delegateCall` target calls back - **ReentrancyGuard** — Uses storage slot to prevent reentrancy, but `nonReentrant` only applied to `execute`, not `executeUserOp` or `selfExecute` — Reentrancy possible via ERC4337 or self-calls - **Guest::_dispatchGuest** — No reentrancy guard — External calls can re-enter wallet - **Simulator::simulate** — No reentrancy guard — Malicious target can re-enter and manipulate state - **Estimator::estimate** — No reentrancy guard — Reentrancy during gas estimation could skew results - **Hooks::fallback** — Delegatecalls to hook without reentrancy protection — Hook can call back into wallet - **Calls::_execute** — `delegateCall` passes control to untrusted extension — Extension can call any function in wallet, bypassing access controls - **Recovery::queuePayload** — Calls `isValidSignature` on `_signer` if code.length > 0 — Reentrancy if signer is malicious contract - **ERC4337v07::validateUserOp** — Calls `isValidSignature` which may call external contracts — Reentrancy during validation - **ERC4337v07::executeUserOp** — Calls `selfExecute` which executes payload — Reentrancy via payload calls - **LibOptim.call / delegatecall (inferred)** — No inline checks on success/revert data — Malicious target can return fake success or revert with manipulated data

CROSS-CHAIN / BRIDGE — - **Payload::domainSeparator** — `_noChainId` flag allows omitting chainId — Signature replayable across chains if noChainId=true - **SessionPermissions::chainId** — If `chainId == 0`, session valid on any chain — Cross-chain replay possible - **ERC4337v07::validateUserOp** — No chainId validation in userOp — Replay on different chains if EntryPoint deployed at same address - **SessionSig::recoverSignature** — `wallet` used in payload hash, but no chainId enforcement — Cross-chain replay if wallet deployed at same address - **Recovery::recoveryPayloadHash** — Uses `_payload.noChainId` — If true, signature valid on all chains

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - **SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature** — Session usage limits tracked per-wallet but not enforced atomically across multiple calls — Attacker can exceed limits by reordering or splitting transactions - **ExplicitSessionManager::incrementUsageLimit** — Caller can increment usage without validation that they consumed it — Griefing by pre-incrementing limits - **ExplicitSessionManager::_validateLimitUsageIncrement** — Expected increment call at index 0, but no validation that it's actually executed first — Attacker can skip increment by setting `onlyFallback` or `behaviorOnError` - **Recovery::queuePayload** — `requiredDeltaTime` can be 0, allowing instant recovery — Attacker can bypass intended delay - **Recovery::_recoverBranch** — `queuedAt` compared to `minTimestamp`, but no check that `minTimestamp` is reasonable — Attacker can set `minTimestamp=0` and bypass queue - **PermissionValidator::validatePermission** — Cumulative limits stored per `keccak256(signer, permission, i)`, but no salt — Collision if same signer+permission used in different sessions - **Nonce::readNonce** — Nonce can be read without incrementing, allowing off-chain simulation — But nonce space can be griefed by front-running with different space - **Calls::_execute** — `call.gasLimit` can be 0, passing all remaining gas — Gas griefing by forcing all gas to be consumed - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — No minimum nonce increment enforced — Attacker can reuse nonce=0 if never incremented (edge case) - **SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature** — `MAX_SPACE = type(uint80).max - 1` but no enforcement that `payload.space <= MAX_SPACE` in payload decoding — Invalid space accepted - **ERC4337v07::validateUserOp** — `missingAccountFunds` deposited to EntryPoint, but no tracking of deposited amount — Attacker can drain wallet by repeatedly calling with high `missingAccountFunds` - **Passkeys::recoverSapientSignatureCompact** — No check on `_requireUserVerification` flag consistency — Attacker can bypass user verification by flipping flag - **WebAuthn (inferred)** — Challenge/response not validated against expected challenge — Replay or cross-origin attacks if challenge reused --- **Note:** Many issues stem from the complex signature recovery logic, lack of input validation on array lengths, and missing reentrancy guards on critical paths. The session management system has multiple economic attack vectors via limit manipulation. Cross-chain replay is possible if `noChainId=true` or `chainId=0`. The reentrancy guard is only applied to `execute`, leaving `executeUserOp`, `selfExecute`, and hook delegatecalls unprotected. ===== CHUNK 2/2 ===== # Findings

ACCESS CONTROL & INIT — - [ACCESS CONTROL] BaseAuth::setStaticSignature — onlySelf modifier allows wallet to set arbitrary static signatures — malicious wallet owner can pre-approve future operations without additional signatures - [ACCESS CONTROL] BaseAuth::updateImageHash — onlySelf allows wallet to change imageHash arbitrarily — wallet can change auth config to malicious one bypassing current signers - [ACCESS CONTROL] Stage1Auth::_updateImageHash — imageHash update automatically switches to STAGE_2_IMPLEMENTATION — if STAGE_2_IMPLEMENTATION is malicious/buggy, wallet is bricked after first update - [ACCESS CONTROL] Stage2Auth::_updateImageHash — no validation that new imageHash corresponds to valid signers — wallet can be locked out if imageHash set to uncontrolled value

ARITHMETIC — - [ARITHMETIC] BaseSig::recoverBranch — unchecked addition `weight += addrWeight` inside unchecked block — could overflow if malicious signature provides many FLAG_SIGNATURE_HASH/FLAG_SIGNATURE_ERC1271 parts - [ARITHMETIC] BaseSig::recoverBranch — unchecked addition `weight += nweight` for FLAG_BRANCH — recursive branch could accumulate weight past uint256 max - [ARITHMETIC] BaseSig::recoverBranch — unchecked addition `weight += externalWeight` for FLAG_NESTED — malicious nested configs can overflow total weight - [ARITHMETIC] BaseSig::recover — checkpoint size read as `(signatureFlag & 0x1c) >> 2` max 7 bytes — if readUintX with size 0 returns dirty data, checkpoint could be wrong - [ARITHMETIC] BaseSig::recover — threshold size `((signatureFlag & 0x20) >> 5) + 1` is 1 or 2 bytes — readUintX with 0 bytes would skip read, threshold could be dirty stack value - [ARITHMETIC] LibBytes::readUintX — `sub(shl(mul(8, _length), 1), 1)` mask — if _length is 0, mask is `sub(1,1) = 0`, returns 0 instead of dirty data - [ARITHMETIC] BaseAuth::_setStaticSignature — `(_timestamp & 0xffffffffffffffffffffffff)` truncates timestamp to 96 bits — timestamp beyond year ~2500 AD wraps around

ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - [INPUT VALIDATION] BaseSig::recover — no bounds check on `checkpointSize` before calling `readUintX(rindex, checkpointSize)` — OOB read if signature crafted with size > remaining bytes - [INPUT VALIDATION] BaseSig::recoverBranch — `rindex < _signature.length` loop condition — if signature malformed, rindex can advance past length, reading dirty data - [INPUT VALIDATION] LibBytes::readUintX — no check that `_index + _length <= _data.length` — reads dirty data beyond calldata if length exceeds remaining bytes - [INPUT VALIDATION] LibBytes::readAddress — no check `_index + 20 <= _data.length` — OOB read returns dirty memory - [INPUT VALIDATION] LibBytes::readBytes32 — no check `_index + 32 <= _data.length` — OOB read returns dirty memory - [INPUT VALIDATION] BaseSig::recoverBranch — FLAG_BRANCH reads size with `readUintX(rindex, sizeSize)` then recurses into `[rindex:nrindex]` — if sizeSize is 0, size is 0, recurses into empty slice, may return garbage root - [INPUT VALIDATION] BaseSig::recoverChained — reads sigSize with `readUint24(rindex)`, then processes `[rindex:nrindex]` — no check that nrindex <= _signature.length, OOB read - [INPUT VALIDATION] BaseSig::recoverBranch — FLAG_NESTED reads size with `readUint24(rindex)` then recurses — no check that nrindex <= _signature.length, OOB read

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - [INCENTIVE] BaseAuth::_getStaticSignature — static signature expires at `timestamp` but uses `block.timestamp` — miner can manipulate block.timestamp to extend or shorten validity window by ~15 seconds - [INCENTIVE] BaseAuth::signatureValidation — static signature with `addr == address(0)` allows any caller — intended as "no restriction", but means anyone can use that operation - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recoverBranch — FLAG_SUBDIGEST sets `weight = type(uint256).max` if `hardcoded == _opHash` — malicious signer could precompute opHash and add subdigest node to always pass threshold - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recoverBranch — FLAG_SIGNATURE_ANY_ADDRESS_SUBDIGEST compares against `_payload.hashFor(address(0))` — allows counter-factual payloads, but if wallet not deployed yet, signature valid for any address - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recoverChained — chained signatures must have decreasing checkpoints — but if attacker controls all signatures, can craft chain that passes validation but represents old config - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recover — snapshot unused if `snapshot.imageHash == imageHash` or `checkpoint > snapshot.checkpoint` — checkpointer can return stale snapshot to make old config appear valid - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recoverBranch — FLAG_NESTED with `internalWeight >= internalThreshold` adds `externalWeight` — attacker can craft nested tree with low internalThreshold to cheaply gain externalWeight - [INCENTIVE] Stage1Auth::_isValidImage — checks wallet address matches `CREATE2(FACTORY, _imageHash, INIT_CODE_HASH)` — if FACTORY compromised, can deploy wallet at predicted address with malicious imageHash - [INCENTIVE] BaseAuth::recoverSapientSignature — appends `msg.sender` to `parentWallets` array — if msg.sender is attacker contract, can inject arbitrary parent into validation chain - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recoverChained — `_snapshot.imageHash = bytes32(0)` after match — if snapshot reused across validations, second validation skips snapshot check - [INCENTIVE] BaseAuth::setStaticSignature — `_timestamp` is uint96 but cast to uint256 in storage encoding — if caller provides timestamp > 2^96, truncation wraps around, signature expires immediately - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recover — threshold read from signature, not storage — attacker can craft signature with threshold=1 even if imageHash encodes higher threshold, bypassing multisig - [INCENTIVE] BaseSig::recoverBranch — multiple FLAG_ADDRESS nodes can reference same address with different weights — imageHash includes each node separately, attacker can reuse address to reduce unique signers needed --- **Summary:** Found ~45 plausible issues across all lenses. Key concerns: unchecked arithmetic in weight accumulation, OOB reads in LibBytes, reentrancy via ERC1271/Sapient callbacks, cross-chain replay via noChainId, static signature expiry manipulation, and threshold bypasses in chained/nested signatures.

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::DEPOSIT_LIMIT — public mutable variable — owner could change it to bypass limit (wait, it's not mutable, but named like a constant) - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::depositTokensToL2 — no fee mechanism — users can deposit/withdraw freely, no incentive for bridge operation - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::withdrawTokensToL1 — signer can approve any withdrawal — malicious signer can drain bridge - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::sendToL1 — no value sent with transaction — if message includes ETH transfer, bridge contract must hold ETH (not funded) - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::DEPOSIT_LIMIT — limit is on vault balance not per-user — whale can block all other deposits - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::withdrawTokensToL1 — no slippage/minAmount parameter — front-running or MEV could manipulate withdrawal value - **[ECONOMIC]** L1Vault — holds all deposited tokens — single point of failure, if compromised all user funds lost - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::depositTokensToL2 — no withdrawal timelock — bridge operator could rug by immediately processing fake withdrawals - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::sendToL1 — arbitrary call with value — bridge needs ETH balance but no deposit function, where does ETH come from? - **[ECONOMIC]** TokenFactory::deployToken — deployed tokens have no link to factory — factory owner can't pause or upgrade deployed tokens - **[ECONOMIC]** L1BossBridge::withdrawTokensToL1 — signature doesn't encode amount commitment — signer could be tricked into signing different amount than user requested ---

Critical Observations — **SIGNATURE REPLAY (Critical):** `sendToL1` has no nonce, no deadline, no chain-id in the signed message — same signature can be replayed infinite times and across chains. **ARBITRARY CALL (Critical):** `sendToL1` allows signers to call ANY function on ANY contract with the bridge's authority — malicious signer can drain vault, steal tokens, or brick contracts. **DEPOSIT LIMIT BYPASS:** DEPOSIT_LIMIT checks vault balance, but withdrawals decrease balance — attacker deposits 100k, withdrawal processed, deposits another 100k, repeat. **CROSS-CHAIN DEPLOYMENT RISK:** ZKSync uses different CREATE/CREATE2 semantics — vault address will differ between Ethereum and ZKSync, breaking bridge assumptions. **NO ETH FUNDING:** `sendToL1` can send ETH (`value` parameter) but bridge has no payable functions — where does ETH come from? Function will always revert if value > 0.

TOKEN ACCOUNTING — - [TOKEN ACCOUNTING] ExplicitSessionManager::VALUE_TRACKING_ADDRESS — uses 0xEeee... for native value but no validation if user sends actual ERC20 at that address — accounting collision - [TOKEN ACCOUNTING] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateExplicitCall — call.value checked against sessionPermissions.valueLimit but no check if wallet actually has balance — could allow phantom value usage

5. ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - [ARRAY] Recovery::_recoverBranch — `while (rindex < _signature.length)` but rindex increments based on data reads — malformed data could skip boundary check - [ARRAY] SessionSig::recoverSignature — `attestationIndex >= attestationList.length` checked but attestationList constructed from untrusted `attestationCount` — could be zero-length and cause revert DOS - [ARRAY] SessionSig::recoverSignature — `callIdx` used to index `payload.calls` and `sig.callSignatures` but no explicit bounds check if they mismatch in length - [ARRAY] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — `payload.calls.length == 0` reverts but sessionUsageLimits array sized to calls.length — edge case if single call - [ARRAY] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateExplicitCall — `permissionIdx >= sessionPermissions.permissions.length` reverts but permissionIdx from untrusted signature — attacker controls revert path - [ARRAY] PermissionValidator::validatePermission — usageLimits array resized with `mstore(newUsageLimits, actualLimitsCount)` — if actualLimitsCount calculation wrong, could point to uninitialized memory - [ARRAY] ImplicitSessionManager::_isAddressBlacklisted — binary search assumes sorted blacklist but only checked in SessionSig::recoverConfiguration — if signature crafted to skip that check, binary search fails - [ARRAY] SessionSig::recoverConfiguration — `while (pointer < encoded.length)` but nested branches recursively call recoverConfiguration — malicious nesting could cause stack overflow or unbounded gas - [ARRAY] Recovery::queuedPayloadHashes — unbounded push to array — no mechanism to clear old entries, eventual DOS on iteration

6. EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY — - [REENTRANCY] ImplicitSessionManager::_validateImplicitCall — external call to `ISignalsImplicitMode(call.to).acceptImplicitRequest` during validation — untrusted target could reenter or manipulate state - [REENTRANCY] Recovery::isValidSignature — calls `IERC1271(_signer).isValidSignature` if signer has code — malicious contract could reenter queuePayload or manipulate timestampForQueuedPayload - [EXTERNAL CALLS] Recovery::queuePayload — calls isValidSignature which can call external IERC1271 before storage write — violates CEI pattern, reentrancy possible - [EXTERNAL CALLS] ImplicitSessionManager::_validateImplicitCall — acceptImplicitRequest call is view but target contract could have state-dependent view that changes between validation and execution

7. CROSS-CHAIN / BRIDGE — - [CROSS-CHAIN] Recovery::domainSeparator — uses `_noChainId` flag to optionally zero chainId — replay attack if user signs with noChainId=true and signature replayed on different chain - [CROSS-CHAIN] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateExplicitCall — sessionPermissions.chainId == 0 means any chain — could allow cross-chain replay if wallet deployed at same address - [CROSS-CHAIN] SessionManager::MAX_SPACE — limits nonce space to uint80 but payload.space is uint256 — inconsistent type could cause issues if migrating cross-chain

8. ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - [ECONOMIC] Recovery::timestampForQueuedPayload — queued timestamp never expires or resets — old queued payloads remain valid forever if requiredDeltaTime passes - [ECONOMIC] Recovery::_recoverBranch — requiredDeltaTime checked with `block.timestamp - queuedAt >= requiredDeltaTime` — miner can manipulate timestamp by ~15 seconds to prematurely allow recovery - [ECONOMIC] ExplicitSessionManager::incrementUsageLimit — allows incrementing usage but no decrement or reset — if user wants to revoke session, usage persists and could hit limits unexpectedly - [ECONOMIC] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateLimitUsageIncrement — requires first call to be incrementUsageLimit if limits exist — user pays gas for increment even if subsequent calls fail, griefing vector - [ECONOMIC] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — validates all calls before any execution — if one call invalid, entire payload fails but user already paid for validation gas - [ECONOMIC] PermissionValidator::validatePermission — cumulative usage tracked per (signer, permission, ruleIndex) hash — same signer with slightly different permission gets separate limit, potential bypass - [ECONOMIC] Recovery::queuePayload — no cost to queue except gas — attacker can queue many payloads for victim's recovery signers to cause high gas cost when victim tries to iterate queuedPayloadHashes - [ECONOMIC] ExplicitSessionManager::VALUE_TRACKING_ADDRESS — native value limit enforced per session but if multiple sessions used in one payload, each has separate limit — could exceed intended total wallet value limit - [ECONOMIC] SessionSig::recoverConfiguration — permissions array pre-allocated to `encoded.length / MIN_ENCODED_PERMISSION_SIZE` — attacker can craft large encoded data with few permissions, wasting memory allocation gas - [ECONOMIC] ImplicitSessionManager::_validateImplicitCall — blacklist checked with binary search O(log n) — attacker can craft maximum-size blacklist to increase validation gas for every call - [ECONOMIC] Passkeys::recoverSapientSignatureCompact — WebAuthn.verify is expensive cryptographic operation — no rate limit, attacker can spam invalid signatures to waste gas - [ECONOMIC] Recovery::_recoverBranch — recursive branch traversal with no depth limit — attacker can craft deeply nested signature to cause high gas or stack overflow - [ECONOMIC] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — loops over all calls, all sessionUsageLimits, all limits — O(n²) or O(n³) complexity, attacker can craft payload with max calls and max limits per session - [ECONOMIC] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateExplicitCall — finds sessionPermissions by linear search through allSessionPermissions — O(n) per call, attacker can add many signers to increase gas - [ECONOMIC] PermissionValidator::validatePermission — loops over usageLimits array for each rule to find matching usageHash — O(rules * limits), gas grief with many rules and limits

Additional High-Severity Concerns — - [CRITICAL] SessionSig::recoverSignature — entire signature structure is user-controlled and parsed without strict validation — malformed encoding could cause reads beyond calldata bounds leading to undefined behavior - [CRITICAL] Recovery::_recoverBranch — verified flag is OR'd across branches but root is built incrementally — attacker could craft signature where one branch verifies (due to old queue) but root doesn't match expected config, accepting invalid signature - [CRITICAL] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateLimitUsageIncrement — constructs expected incrementUsageLimit call data and compares hash — attacker could frontrun with different call data that changes limits before comparison, bypassing validation - [CRITICAL] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — returns sig.imageHash but imageHash is built from user-controlled signature — if signature parsing has bug, wrong imageHash returned and arbitrary config accepted - [CRITICAL] ImplicitSessionManager::_isAddressBlacklisted — binary search assumes sorted array but sorting only validated once during recoverConfiguration — if memory corruption or assembly error, search returns wrong result - [CRITICAL] Passkeys::_decodeSignature — flags byte determines encoding path but 0x20 bit determines abi.decode fallback — attacker can flip bit to bypass compact encoding checks and inject arbitrary x, y coords

H-01 Chained signature path bypasses checkpointer when chained-mode flag set — Control-flow gate where a flag bypasses signature-validation chain. Chained-sig branch never invokes checkpointer guard. Citation: human_only (novel structural shape; candidate for between-contest TLA+ corpus growth).

H-02 Partial signature replay across session sub-calls — SessionSig validates each per-call signature independently without binding to batch identity / nonce / index. Citation: TLC counterexample on new spec PartialSignatureReplay.tla — Invariant BatchIntegrity violated at state 2 with SubmitBuggy(c=1, b=2).

M-01 Session signatures replay across wallets due to missing wallet binding — SessionManager-derived signing payload omits address(this) from EIP-712 domain. Citation: human_only (close to ERC4337StaticSigDoS shape but distinct; candidate for between-contest extension).

M-02 Static signatures bound to caller revert under ERC-4337 EntryPoint — msg.sender check fails on 4337 path. Citation: TLC counterexample on existing docs/tla/ERC4337StaticSigDoS.tla — Invariant Authorized4337CallsExecute violated.

M-03 BaseAuth.recoverSapientSignature returns hard-coded constant — Function body returns sentinel instead of recovered signer image hash. Citation: human_only (TLA+ structurally cannot model "function returns wrong constant").

M-04 Factory.deploy reverts when contract already exists instead of returning address — CREATE2 path lacks extcodesize pre-check. Citation: TLC counterexample on docs/tla/Create2NonIdempotent.tla — Invariant DeployedNeverReverts violated.
L-01 Incorrect intermediate validation of cumulative parameter rules — Per-call cumulative check uses wrong accumulator state. Citation: human_only.
L-02 Session value-spent counter increments on fallback and reverted calls — Counter-on-failed-path. Citation: human_only.

L-03 Nonce consumption reverts on execution failure enabling signature replay — Nonce intended as one-shot guard but update reverts with inner call. Citation: human_only (close to SignatureReplay shape; candidate for variant).
L-04 Unnecessary bitmasking in LibBytes::readUintX — Defensive bitmasks redundant. Citation: slither incorrect-shift detector at utils/LibBytes.sol:76.

From NatSpec Comments — **P1**: "redeem(mint(x)) returns exactly x" — *Not explicitly stated, but I'll search for any round-trip guarantees* **P2**: `Factory::deploy` — "Deploy a new wallet instance" returning `_contract` address. No explicit promise about uniqueness or determinism beyond using CREATE2 with salt. **P3**: `ERC4337FactoryWrapper::deploy` — "Only the senderCreator can deploy a wallet" (line 30: "Only the senderCreator can deploy a wallet.") **P4**: `Wallet` proxy — The Huff-documented delegate proxy promises: "Calls with calldata (with or without ETH value) are forwarded to the stored target. Calls sending only ETH without calldata do nothing and return immediately without forwarding." **P5**: `Estimator::estimate` — "Estimate the gas used for payload validation and execution" returning `gasUsed` (line 41: "return startingGas - gasleft()") **P6**: `Simulator::simulate` — "Simulate the execution of a payload" returning `Result[] memory results` with status for each call **P7**: `SessionManager::MAX_SPACE` — "Maximum nonce space allowed for sessions use" (line 18: "uint256 public constant MAX_SPACE = type(uint80).max - 1") **P8**: Session validation — Sessions must validate: chainId (if set), deadline (if set), blacklist (for implicit), permissions (for explicit), and value limits **P9**: `ExplicitSessionManager::incrementUsageLimit` — "Increment usage for a caller's given session and target" with the constraint "Cannot decrement usage limit" (SessionErrors line 27: "revert SessionErrors.InvalidLimitUsageIncrement()") **P10**: `Calls::execute` — Executes calls according to their `behaviorOnError`: IGNORE_ERROR continues with errorFlag, REVERT_ON_ERROR reverts entire transaction, ABORT_ON_ERROR stops processing remaining calls **P11**: Nonce consumption — Each payload's nonce is consumed exactly once (via `_consumeNonce`) **P12**: ERC4337 validation — `validateUserOp` returns 0 for valid signatures, SIG_VALIDATION_FAILED (1) for invalid (line 34: "return SIG_VALIDATION_FAILED") **P13**: Reentrancy protection — All external entry points are protected by `nonReentrant` modifier **P14**: WebAuthn verification — `WebAuthn.verify` validates: authenticatorData with User Present bit (bit 0), User Verified bit (bit 2) if required, challenge match in clientDataJSON, valid P256 signature **P15**: Recovery queuing — "Queue a payload for recovery" with timestamp, allowing execution after `requiredDeltaTime` (line 146: "block.timestamp - queuedAt >= requiredDeltaTime")
From README — **P16**: No explicit functional promises in README beyond setup instructions.
From ADRs — **P17**: No ADRs found — no additional promises. --- # Violations — where the code betrays them

No mechanistic violations found — After exhaustive analysis of all promises against the actual code with the struggle map showing uniform 1-commit history (no debugging hotspots), I attempted to construct mechanistic proofs for potential violations:

P1: Nonce Sequentiality — **Source:** `Nonce.sol` NatSpec comments and implementation **Promise:** "The nonce must increment sequentially by exactly 1 for each consumed nonce in a given space. `readNonce(_space)` must equal `_nonce` before consumption, and must equal `_nonce + 1` after consumption."

P2: ERC1271 Signature Validation — **Source:** `IERC1271.sol` interface documentation **Promise:** "MUST return the correct magic value if the signature provided is valid for the provided hash. The bytes4 magic value to return when signature is valid is 0x1626ba7e"

P3: Reentrancy Protection — **Source:** `ReentrancyGuard.sol` modifier documentation **Promise:** "Prevents a contract from calling itself, directly or indirectly. Any calls to nonReentrant after the first will fail with ReentrantCall error."

P4: Implementation Address Storage — **Source:** `Implementation.sol` comments and implementation **Promise:** "The implementation address is stored at storage slot `address()` (slot 0 cast to bytes32). `getImplementation()` must return the value stored at this slot."

P5: Image Hash Validation (Stage1) — **Source:** `Stage1Auth.sol` `_isValidImage` implementation **Promise:** "For Stage1, an imageHash is valid if and only if `address(uint160(uint256(keccak256(abi.encodePacked(hex"ff", FACTORY, _imageHash, INIT_CODE_HASH))))) == address(this)`. This implements CREATE2 address validation."

P6: Image Hash Validation (Stage2) — **Source:** `Stage2Auth.sol` `_isValidImage` implementation **Promise:** "For Stage2, an imageHash is valid if and only if it is non-zero AND equals the value stored at IMAGE_HASH_KEY storage slot."

P7: Image Hash Non-Zero Constraint — **Source:** `Stage1Auth.sol` and `Stage2Auth.sol` `_updateImageHash` functions **Promise:** "Image hash must never be set to bytes32(0). Attempting to set it to zero must revert with ImageHashIsZero error."

P8: Signature Weight Threshold — **Source:** `BaseSig.sol` `recover` function and error definitions **Promise:** "For a signature to be valid, the recovered weight must be >= threshold. If weight < threshold, the function must revert with InvalidSignatureWeight or LowWeightChainedSignature error."

P9: EIP-712 Domain Separator — **Source:** `Payload.sol` `domainSeparator` function **Promise:** "Domain separator must be computed as keccak256(abi.encode(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, 'Sequence Wallet' name hash, '3' version hash, chainId (or 0 if noChainId), wallet address))"

P10: Static Signature Expiration — **Source:** `BaseAuth.sol` `signatureValidation` function **Promise:** "Static signatures must only be valid while block.timestamp < stored timestamp. If timestamp <= block.timestamp, must revert with InvalidStaticSignatureExpired error."

P11: Checkpoint Ordering in Chained Signatures — **Source:** `BaseSig.sol` `recoverChained` function **Promise:** "In chained signatures, each subsequent checkpoint must be strictly less than the previous checkpoint. If checkpoint >= prevCheckpoint, must revert with WrongChainedCheckpointOrder."

P12: Snapshot Validation — **Source:** `BaseSig.sol` recovery functions **Promise:** "If a snapshot is provided with non-zero imageHash, either the recovered imageHash must match the snapshot's imageHash, OR the checkpoint must be > snapshot.checkpoint. Otherwise, must revert with UnusedSnapshot error."

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P1 (Nonce Sequentiality) — The `_consumeNonce` function correctly validates `currentNonce == _nonce` before incrementing by 1. The unchecked block is safe because nonce overflow at 2^256-1 is not a realistic attack vector.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P2 (ERC1271 Signature Validation) — The `isValidSignature` function returns `IERC1271_MAGIC_VALUE_HASH` (0x1626ba7e) on success and `bytes4(0)` on failure, exactly as specified.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P3 (Reentrancy Protection) — The `nonReentrant` modifier correctly sets status to `_ENTERED` before execution and resets to `_NOT_ENTERED` after, preventing reentrant calls.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P4 (Implementation Address Storage) — `_setImplementation` stores at slot `address()` and `_getImplementation` reads from the same slot. The pattern is consistent.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P5 (Image Hash Validation Stage1) — The CREATE2 address computation in `_isValidImage` correctly uses `keccak256(abi.encodePacked(hex"ff", FACTORY, _imageHash, INIT_CODE_HASH))` and compares the derived address to `address(this)`.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P6 (Image Hash Validation Stage2) — `_isValidImage` returns `_imageHash != bytes32(0) && _imageHash == Storage.readBytes32(IMAGE_HASH_KEY)`, which matches the promise.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P7 (Image Hash Non-Zero Constraint) — Both Stage1Auth and Stage2Auth `_updateImageHash` functions check `if (_imageHash == bytes32(0)) revert ImageHashIsZero()` before writing to storage.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P8 (Signature Weight Threshold) — In `BaseSig.recover`, line 87-89 validates `if (weight < threshold) revert InvalidSignatureWeight(threshold, weight)`. In `recoverChained`, line 128-130 validates each link.
No mechanistic violation found for Promise P9 (EIP-712 Domain Separator) — The `domainSeparator` function at lines 43-52 of Payload.sol correctly encodes all required fields with proper constants.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P10 (Static Signature Expiration) — `BaseAuth.sol` lines 77-79 check `if (timestamp <= block.timestamp) revert InvalidStaticSignatureExpired(opHash, timestamp)`, enforcing strict future expiration.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P11 (Checkpoint Ordering in Chained Signatures) — `BaseSig.recoverChained` lines 142-144 enforce `if (checkpoint >= prevCheckpoint) revert WrongChainedCheckpointOrder(checkpoint, prevCheckpoint)`.

No mechanistic violation found for Promise P12 (Snapshot Validation) — Lines 101-103 of `BaseSig.recover` and lines 148-150 of `recoverChained` both check the snapshot constraint: `if (snapshot.imageHash != bytes32(0) && snapshot.imageHash != imageHash && checkpoint <= snapshot.checkpoint) revert UnusedSnapshot(snapshot)`. ---

ACCESS CONTROL & INIT — - PuppyRaffle::changeFeeAddress — No zero-address validation on newFeeAddress — Owner could accidentally set feeAddress to zero, causing fee withdrawals to fail or burn funds - PuppyRaffle::constructor — feeAddress not validated against zero — Initial deployment could have zero feeAddress, breaking fee withdrawal logic - PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees — Not protected by onlyOwner or role check — Anyone can trigger fee withdrawal to feeAddress (though funds go to correct address, could be griefed)
ORACLE / PRICE FEED — - None found (protocol does not use oracles or price feeds)

ARITHMETIC — - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — totalFees += uint64(fee) can overflow uint64 — If entranceFee is high or many rounds occur, casting fee to uint64 truncates high bits, losing fee tracking accuracy - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — prizePool = (totalAmountCollected * 80) / 100 rounding down — Winner loses wei due to truncation, favors protocol - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — fee = (totalAmountCollected * 20) / 100 rounding down — Combined with prizePool rounding, dust remains in contract - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — No check that totalAmountCollected = prizePool + fee — Rounding could cause prizePool + fee < totalAmountCollected, leaving dust locked - PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees — require(address(this).balance == uint256(totalFees)) can fail if dust from rounding remains — Fees become unwithdrawable if any wei remains from prizePool/fee split - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — Unchecked totalFees = totalFees + uint64(fee) wraps on overflow — Solidity 0.7.6 doesn't have default overflow checks; uint64 can silently wrap

TOKEN ACCOUNTING — - PuppyRaffle::refund — sendValue does not check for reentrancy before state update — payable(msg.sender).sendValue(entranceFee) called before players[playerIndex] = address(0), violates CEI - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — winner.call{value: prizePool} before _safeMint — Reentrancy possible: winner can reenter before NFT is minted - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — _safeMint can call onERC721Received on winner — Winner can reenter during callback before raffle state fully reset - PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees — feeAddress.call{value:} without reentrancy guard — feeAddress (controlled by owner) could reenter if malicious - PuppyRaffle::refund — No check if entranceFee refund will drain contract below totalFees — Could refund fees that should be reserved, breaking withdrawFees invariant

ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - PuppyRaffle::refund — playerIndex not bounds-checked against players.length — Out-of-bounds access reverts but wastes gas; intentional DoS vector - PuppyRaffle::getActivePlayerIndex — Returns 0 for both "not found" and "player at index 0" — Ambiguous return: cannot distinguish player at index 0 from absent player - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — No upper bound on newPlayers.length — Attacker can pass massive array, causing quadratic duplicate-check loop to DoS the function - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — Duplicate check is O(n²) on players.length — Gas cost grows quadratically; later entrants pay exponentially more gas - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — Requires players.length >= 4, butwinnerIndex = hash % players.length — If players.length changes between check and use (impossible here but pattern is fragile) - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — No check that newPlayers addresses are non-zero — Players can enter address(0), which refund() then disallows, creating unrefundable slot

EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY — - PuppyRaffle::refund — sendValue(entranceFee) before players[playerIndex] = address(0) — Classic CEI violation: attacker can reenter refund via fallback and drain multiple times - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — winner.call{value: prizePool} before deleting players — Winner can reenter selectWinner, double-claim prizePool if players not cleared - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — External call winner.call before state reset (delete players, raffleStartTime) — Reentrancy allows winner to call selectWinner again in same raffle round - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — _safeMint calls onERC721Received callback — Winner can reenter protocol during mint, potentially before raffle state is finalized - PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees — feeAddress.call{value:} with no reentrancy guard — If feeAddress is malicious contract, can reenter and exploit state - PuppyRaffle::refund — Address.sendValue uses call, not transfer — Forwards all gas, enabling reentrancy attacks
CROSS-CHAIN / BRIDGE — - None found (single-chain raffle, no bridge logic)

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — Weak randomness: keccak256(msg.sender, block.timestamp, block.difficulty) — Miner or last caller can manipulate block.difficulty and timestamp to influence winner - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — msg.sender in RNG seed — Attacker can choose when to call selectWinner to bias outcome in their favor - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — Rarity RNG uses msg.sender, block.difficulty (no block.timestamp) — Different seed from winner RNG but still miner-influenceable; can bias rare NFT outcomes - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — No minimum prize pool or fee check — If entranceFee is 1 wei and 4 players, prizePool and fee round to zero, winner gets nothing - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — Players can enter address(0) or duplicate in newPlayers before pushing to storage — newPlayers list not validated before pushing; only checked after, wasting gas - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — Duplicate check compares all players including old ones — Front-running: attacker can enter before victim, causing victim's tx to revert on duplicate, griefing - PuppyRaffle::getActivePlayerIndex — Returns 0 for "not found" — Caller cannot distinguish between legitimate index 0 and absence; could trick refund logic - PuppyRaffle::refund — Refunded player leaves address(0) gap in array — Array becomes sparse; selectWinner picks from all indices including gaps (address(0) could "win") - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — Winner selected from players array that may contain address(0) — If winner == address(0), prize sent to zero address (burned) and NFT mint may fail or burn - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — previousWinner set to winner after call — No event or logic protecting previousWinner from being overwritten maliciously - PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees — Exact balance check require(address(this).balance == uint256(totalFees)) — Impossible to satisfy if anyone force-sends ETH via selfdestruct, locking fees forever - PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees — totalFees is uint64 but feesToWithdraw cast to uint256 — If totalFees overflowed and wrapped, feesToWithdraw is wrong amount (though uint64->uint256 is safe, the source overflow is the issue) - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — Duplicate check runs after pushing newPlayers to storage — If duplicate found, transaction reverts but gas spent on SSTORE; griefing vector - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — delete players resets to empty array but no event logging old players — Off-chain indexers cannot track who was in the final raffle round - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — raffleStartTime reset to block.timestamp without checking old timestamp — Back-to-back selectWinner calls could reset duration unexpectedly if called at exact boundary - PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle — msg.value == entranceFee * newPlayers.length — No refund of excess ETH if user overpays; strict equality means overpayment reverts - PuppyRaffle::refund — emit RaffleRefunded(playerAddress) after sendValue — Event emitted even if sendValue fails (it reverts, so unreachable, but pattern is post-call) - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — No slippage or minimum prizePool protection — Winner could receive far less than expected if entranceFee is tiny or totalAmountCollected rounds badly - PuppyRaffle::totalFees — Storage packing with feeAddress and uint64 totalFees — If totalFees overflows uint64, subsequent fee accounting is broken; feeAddress SSTORE also touches totalFees slot (gas consideration, not bug per se) - PuppyRaffle::changeFeeAddress — No timelockOrDelay or two-step transfer — Owner can rug by changing feeAddress to own wallet right before withdrawFees - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — No check that winner != address(0) before sending prizePool — If winner is address(0) (from refund gap), ETH sent to zero address is burned - PuppyRaffle::selectWinner — totalSupply() used as tokenId — If NFTs are burned or transferred, totalSupply may not equal next tokenId; collision or skipped IDs possible (ERC721Enumerable issue) - PuppyRaffle::tokenIdToRarity mapping — tokenId from totalSupply() may collide if NFTs burned — Burning and re-minting could overwrite rarity for an existing token --- **Summary**: 40+ distinct issues across all lenses, with most critical in reentrancy (CEI violations in refund/selectWinner), weak RNG (miner manipulation), arithmetic (uint64 overflow on totalFees, rounding leaving dust, impossible withdrawFees condition), and economic griefing (quadratic duplicate check DoS, address(0) winner, force-send bricking withdrawFees).

1. ACCESS CONTROL & INIT — - [ACCESS CONTROL] TSwapPool::constructor — no validation on poolToken or wethToken addresses — could be set to address(0) or duplicate addresses - [ACCESS CONTROL] PoolFactory::constructor — no validation on wethToken address — could be set to address(0) - [ACCESS CONTROL] PoolFactory::createPool — anyone can create pools with arbitrary tokens — could create pools with malicious/fake tokens - [ACCESS CONTROL] TSwapPool::deposit — no deadline check enforced on first deposit — unlike subsequent deposits, initial deposit ignores deadline parameter completely

2. ORACLE / PRICE FEED — - [ORACLE] TSwapPool::getPriceOfOneWethInPoolTokens — uses spot reserves without TWAP — vulnerable to flash-loan manipulation - [ORACLE] TSwapPool::getPriceOfOnePoolTokenInWeth — uses spot reserves without TWAP — vulnerable to flash-loan manipulation - [ORACLE] TSwapPool::getOutputAmountBasedOnInput — no staleness check — price can be stale if pool hasn't traded recently - [ORACLE] TSwapPool::deposit — uses balanceOf for reserves immediately after user could manipulate — donation attack vector before first deposit

ARITHMETIC — - [ARITHMETIC] TSwapPool::getOutputAmountBasedOnInput — division rounds down favoring protocol — user gets less output than mathematically fair - [ARITHMETIC] TSwapPool::getInputAmountBasedOnOutput — division rounds down then multiplies by 10000/997 — asymmetric rounding vs getOutputAmountBasedOnInput - [ARITHMETIC] TSwapPool::withdraw — liquidityTokensToBurn * balance / totalSupply rounds down — user loses dust on every withdraw - [ARITHMETIC] TSwapPool::deposit — liquidityTokensToMint calculation rounds down — user gets fewer LP tokens than fair share - [ARITHMETIC] TSwapPool::getPoolTokensToDepositBasedOnWeth — division truncates — poolTokensToDeposit could be 1 wei less than needed to maintain ratio - [ARITHMETIC] TSwapPool::getInputAmountBasedOnOutput — (inputReserves * outputAmount) could overflow for large reserves and amounts — no overflow protection in Solidity 0.8.20 only on multiplication before division

4. ARITHMETIC — - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `unchecked` block in loop incrementing `pointer` — overflow would cause pointer to wrap, reading wrong calldata positions or reverting - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `(globalFlag >> 1) & 0x07` for nonceSize, then `readUintX(pointer, nonceSize)` — if nonceSize=0 and readUintX mishandles, could read garbage - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `(flags & 0xC0) >> 6` for behaviorOnError — if flags are malformed, behaviorOnError could be 3 (undefined), causing unexpected control flow - **BaseSig::recoverBranch** — `unchecked { uint256 newNonce = _nonce + 1; }` in `_consumeNonce` — nonce overflow at max uint256 would wrap to 0, allowing replay of nonce=0 transactions - **BaseSig::recoverBranch** — weight accumulation `weight += addrWeight` in loop — could overflow if many signers, causing weight to wrap and bypass threshold check - **BaseSig::recoverChained** — `checkpoint >= prevCheckpoint` check — off-by-one: should be strictly less-than to prevent same checkpoint reuse, but uses `>=` which forbids equality (correct), yet doesn't prevent checkpoint=0 reuse if prevCheckpoint starts at max - **LibBytes::readUintX** — `sub(shl(mul(8, _length), 1), 1)` mask — if `_length` is 0, `mul(8,0)=0`, `shl(0,1)=1`, `sub(1,1)=0`, mask is 0, always returns 0 (silent failure) - **LibBytes::readUintX** — no bounds check on `_length` — if `_length>32`, shift overflows, mask is wrong, reads garbage - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `numCalls` read as uint16 or uint8, max 65535 or 255 — if actual calls exceed, pointer arithmetic breaks, out-of-gas or wrong data - **Payload::hashCalls** — loop creates `bytes32[] memory callHashes` of length `calls.length` — unbounded array allocation, massive gas or revert if calls.length is huge

CROSS-CHAIN / BRIDGE — - **Payload::domainSeparator** — `_noChainId ? uint256(0) : uint256(block.chainid)` — if `_noChainId=true`, same signature valid on all chains (replay attack across chains) - **Payload::hashFor** — `_wallet` parameter allows computing hash for arbitrary wallet — could be used to craft cross-wallet replay if imageHash or config is shared - **Stage1Auth::_isValidImage** — uses `FACTORY` and `INIT_CODE_HASH` immutables — if factory is deployed at different address on different chain, imageHash validation breaks (wallet locked) - **Wallet::creationCode** — deterministic deploy via create2 — if salt or module differ across chains, same wallet address has different code, cross-chain assumptions fail - **BaseSig::recoverChained** — chained signatures with `_checkpointer` — if checkpointer is not deployed or differs across chains, chained replay may succeed/fail unpredictably

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - **Calls::_execute** — `gasLimit == 0 ? gasleft()` — if gasLimit=0, forwards all remaining gas; attacker can craft low-gas call to force out-of-gas on subsequent calls (griefing) - **Calls::_execute** — `BEHAVIOR_ABORT_ON_ERROR` stops loop — if call[0] set to abort, attacker can prevent execution of call[1..N], DoS legitimate transactions - **Calls::_execute** — `onlyFallback` flag — if errorFlag is manipulated, onlyFallback calls can be skipped or executed wrongly, bypassing intended fallback logic - **ERC4337v07::validateUserOp** — `missingAccountFunds` deposited to entrypoint — if userOp is crafted with high `missingAccountFunds`, wallet drains its ETH to entrypoint, attacker can grief - **BaseAuth::signatureValidation** — static signature with `timestamp <= block.timestamp` always valid — if timestamp is far future, signature valid indefinitely, no rotation possible - **BaseAuth::signatureValidation** — static signature with `addr == address(0)` accepts any caller — open to front-running, anyone can execute if signature is leaked - **BaseSig::recoverBranch** — `weight = type(uint256).max` on subdigest match — attacker can precompute `anyAddressOpHash` or hardcoded subdigest to bypass all weight checks (inflation attack) - **Nonce::_consumeNonce** — nonce incremented by 1 — if nonce=max, overflows to 0 (unchecked block), old nonce=0 transactions can replay - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — space can be 0 or arbitrary — if space=0 is special, attacker can reset nonce space to 0, replay old space=0 transactions - **Stage1Auth::_updateImageHash** — updates to stage2 implementation — if stage2 has vulnerability, all wallets auto-upgrade to vulnerable code, systemic risk - **Hooks::addHook** — no limit on number of hooks — attacker can add infinite hooks, DoS fallback calls via out-of-gas - **BaseSig::recoverChained** — `weight < threshold` check in loop — if weight is exactly threshold-1, loop continues, next signature can push over, but if all signatures are threshold-1, final imageHash may be invalid yet weight summed (logic unclear, potential bypass) --- **Note**: Many of these are speculative or edge-case concerns. Downstream filtering will assess exploitability and impact. ===== CHUNK 2/2 ===== # Findings

[ECONOMIC] TSwapPool::sellPoolTokens — function name implies selling but actually buying — user confusion, they think they're selling poolToken but getting poolToken out - [ECONOMIC] TSwapPool::sellPoolTokens — swapExactOutput with poolTokenAmount as outputAmount — user wants to sell X poolTokens, function tries to output X poolTokens (buys instead of sells) - [ECONOMIC] TSwapPool::_swap — bonus paid in outputToken — if outputToken is WETH (valuable), attacker drains WETH; if poolToken (less liquid), attacker dumps on market ---

[SIGNATURE REPLAY] L1BossBridge::sendToL1 — signature has no nonce, expiry, or used-tracking — infinite replay attack; attacker can drain vault with single valid signature
[SIGNATURE MALLEABILITY] L1BossBridge::sendToL1 — uses ECDSA.recover without checking s value — signature malleability possible (though OpenZeppelin ECDSA mitigates this)
[ARBITRARY EXECUTION] L1BossBridge::sendToL1 — signer can execute arbitrary calls as bridge — signer can call vault.approveTo to approve attacker, then steal all tokens
[CENTRALIZATION] L1BossBridge — single owner controls pause and signers — owner can pause indefinitely, trapping funds; or add malicious signer
[VAULT OWNERSHIP] L1Vault — created with msg.sender as owner then should transfer to bridge — if ownership transfer not done atomically in constructor, bridge may not own vault
[CONSTRUCTOR ISSUE] L1BossBridge::constructor — vault created but ownership not transferred — vault.owner() is bridge deployer, not bridge contract itself
[WRONG FROM] L1BossBridge::depositTokensToL2 — takes 'from' parameter but caller could be different — allows anyone to deposit from any address with approval
[NO REFUND] L1BossBridge::depositTokensToL2 — if deposit fails on L2, no refund mechanism — user loses L1 tokens with no L2 credit
[ETH HANDLING] L1BossBridge::sendToL1 — sends ETH via value but bridge has no ETH source — call will fail if value > 0 and bridge has no ETH balance
[DECIMALS MISMATCH] L1Token — 18 decimals assumed everywhere — if TokenFactory deploys token with different decimals, DEPOSIT_LIMIT and accounting breaks

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - **Calls::_execute** — `gasLimit == 0 ? gasleft() : gasLimit` — if gasLimit=0, forwards all remaining gas, last call can consume everything and DOS later calls - **Estimator::estimate** — returns `startingGas - gasleft()` — if called with low gas, returns inflated estimate - **Simulator::simulate** — `gasUsed` tracked per call but no aggregate limit — simulation could OOG on large call array - **BaseAuth::setStaticSignature** — static signature with timestamp allows bypassing signature validation until expiry — frontrunning attack: set static sig, execute, remove sig in same block - **BaseSig::recoverChained** — chained signatures validated in order, weight from first checked against threshold — if first signature is weak (threshold=1, weight=1), chain is accepted even if later signatures invalid - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — `globalFlag` bit 5 determines single call vs batch — single-call optimization skips reading numCalls, but no gas refund difference - **Nonce::_consumeNonce** — nonce increment emits NonceChange event — griefing: malicious actor can increment nonce to max, forcing wait for overflow - **ERC4337v07::validateUserOp** — `missingAccountFunds` deposited even if signature invalid — griefing: invalid userOp consumes wallet ETH to pay entrypoint ---

Additional observations (high-level risks): — - **Calls::_execute** — `errorFlag` reset per call, `onlyFallback` only applies to immediately following call — complex error cascades could be confusing, off-by-one error in recovery logic - **BaseSig::recoverChained** — `prevCheckpoint` must decrease monotonically — but if checkpointer returns wrong snapshot, chain validation breaks - **Stage1Auth::_isValidImage** — validates imageHash via counterfactual address == address(this) — if factory or init code changes, old wallets become invalid - **Hooks::fallback** — hook delegatecall result returned directly — if hook returns large data, could OOG on return copy - **Payload::fromPackedCalls** — compressed encoding with flags and variable-length fields — malformed encoding could cause parsing to fail silently or read garbage ===== CHUNK 2/2 ===== # Findings

1. ARITHMETIC — - **[ARITH]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — `totalFees = totalFees + uint64(fee)` downcasts uint256 to uint64 — fee calculation can overflow uint64, causing totalFees to wrap and lose value - **[ARITH]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — prizePool and fee calculated with division before multiplication pattern — rounding could cause prizePool + fee != totalAmountCollected, leaving dust in contract - **[ARITH]** `PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle` — `msg.value == entranceFee * newPlayers.length` unchecked multiplication — large newPlayers.length could overflow and allow entry with insufficient payment - **[ARITH]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — `players.length * entranceFee` unchecked multiplication — overflow possible if many players, corrupting totalAmountCollected

4. TOKEN ACCOUNTING — - **[TOKEN]** `PuppyRaffle::refund` — uses `sendValue` but doesn't follow CEI pattern — state updated after external call, reentrancy vector - **[TOKEN]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — sends prizePool before minting NFT — reentrancy possible if winner is contract with receive() that calls back - **[TOKEN]** `PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees` — `require(address(this).balance == uint256(totalFees))` strict equality — can be DoS'd by sending 1 wei to contract, breaking equality check - **[TOKEN]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — _safeMint at end after external call — reentrancy risk, winner could reenter before mint completes

5. ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - **[ARRAY]** `PuppyRaffle::refund` — playerIndex not validated against players.length — out of bounds access possible causing revert or worse - **[ARRAY]** `PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle` — duplicate check is O(n²) — DoS vector as gas cost grows quadratically with player count - **[ARRAY]** `PuppyRaffle::getActivePlayerIndex` — returns 0 for not found AND for index 0 — ambiguous return value, player at index 0 indistinguishable from not found - **[ARRAY]** `PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle` — no maximum on newPlayers.length — attacker can pass huge array causing out-of-gas DoS - **[ARRAY]** `PuppyRaffle::refund` — sets players[playerIndex] to address(0) creating gaps — winner selection can pick address(0), breaking payout logic

6. EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY — - **[REENTRANCY]** `PuppyRaffle::refund` — payable(msg.sender).sendValue(entranceFee) before zeroing array slot — classic reentrancy, attacker can drain via repeated refund calls - **[REENTRANCY]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — winner.call{value: prizePool} before _safeMint — winner contract can reenter selectWinner before state fully settled - **[REENTRANCY]** `PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees` — feeAddress.call before balance check completes — if feeAddress is malicious contract, can reenter - **[EXTCALL]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — (bool success,) = winner.call{value:} with require on success — if winner is contract that reverts, raffle is stuck and cannot select new winner - **[EXTCALL]** `PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees` — relies on call success to feeAddress — malicious/broken feeAddress can permanently lock fees

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — uses block.timestamp and block.difficulty in randomness — miner/validator can manipulate to influence winner selection - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — uses msg.sender in randomness — caller can grind transaction to manipulate winner selection - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — winner selection includes address(0) slots from refunds — if winner is address(0), prizePool is burned, no one wins - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle` — no slippage protection on entry — attacker can frontrun with refund to change player count, affecting expected position - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::refund` — no time-lock or penalty — players can enter/exit freely, gaming the system based on participant count - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::withdrawFees` — strict equality check blocks withdrawal if ANY eth donated — griefer can send 1 wei to permanently lock all fees - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — requires exactly 4 players minimum — if 4th player refunds before selectWinner, raffle cannot complete - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::totalFees` — uint64 storage while fees are uint256 — over time fees will overflow and wrap, causing accounting mismatch - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — rarity calculation uses different RNG than winner — rarity is more manipulable since it doesn't include timestamp - **[ECONOMIC]** `PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle` — no limit on entries per address in newPlayers array — single user can fill entire raffle, centralizing win probability

ADDITIONAL OBSERVATIONS — - **[GAS]** `PuppyRaffle::enterRaffle` — O(n²) duplicate check scales terribly — later entrants pay exponentially more gas - **[GAS]** `PuppyRaffle::_isActivePlayer` — O(n) loop on every check — expensive for large player counts - **[GAS]** `PuppyRaffle::getActivePlayerIndex` — O(n) loop — expensive operation that doesn't scale - **[DESIGN]** `PuppyRaffle::refund` — creates sparse array with address(0) — breaks assumptions about players array being packed with valid addresses - **[DESIGN]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — delete players resets to empty array but keeps address(0) slots until then — winner could be invalid address - **[MEV]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — public function with value transfer — MEV bots can frontrun to ensure they trigger the win selection at optimal time - **[PRECISION]** `PuppyRaffle::selectWinner` — 80/20 split hardcoded with integer division — assumes entranceFee * players.length divisible by 100, else rounding issues

CRITICAL ISSUES SUMMARY — The most severe issues found: 1. **Signature replay attack** (BRIDGE) - No nonce/replay protection allows infinite withdrawals with single signature 2. **Cross-chain replay** (BRIDGE) - Same signature valid on Ethereum and ZKSync allows double-withdrawal 3. **Arbitrary execution** (ACCESS CONTROL) - Signer can call any function on any contract via sendToL1 4. **Vault owner confusion** (ACCESS CONTROL) - Vault owner is not the bridge contract as intended 5. **DEPOSIT_LIMIT never decreases** (BRIDGE) - Bridge becomes unusable after first 100k deposits 6. **Fee-on-transfer token mismatch** (TOKEN ACCOUNTING) - Though out of scope, violates L1/L2 balance invariant

ACCESS CONTROL & INIT — - TSwapPool::constructor — no validation of pool/weth token addresses — could deploy with zero addresses or duplicate tokens leading to broken pool - PoolFactory::constructor — no validation of wethToken address — zero address would break all pool creation - TSwapPool::deposit — first depositor sets initial ratio with no slippage protection — attacker can deposit 1 wei WETH + huge poolToken amount to manipulate all future deposits - TSwapPool::_addLiquidityMintAndTransfer — private function but no CEI pattern; mint before transfer — reentrancy on ERC777 could double-mint LP tokens

ORACLE / PRICE FEED — - TSwapPool::getPriceOfOneWethInPoolTokens — uses spot reserves with no TWAP — flashloan can manipulate price oracle for external integrators - TSwapPool::getPriceOfOnePoolTokenInWeth — same spot price manipulation risk — external contracts trusting this could be drained - TSwapPool::swapExactInput — uses balanceOf for reserves AFTER user transfer — if called in same block as previous swap, includes pending balance - TSwapPool::swapExactOutput — same reserve reading issue — can be manipulated by ordering transactions in block

ARITHMETIC — - TSwapPool::getOutputAmountBasedOnInput — division truncation favors protocol — user receives less output than formula suggests - TSwapPool::getInputAmountBasedOnOutput — multiplication then division; intermediate overflow possible if reserves/amounts huge — could revert legitimate large trades - TSwapPool::deposit — liquidityTokensToMint uses division, rounds down — depositor loses fractional LP tokens on every deposit - TSwapPool::withdraw — wethToWithdraw & poolTokensToWithdraw both divide, round down — user receives less than proportional share - TSwapPool::getInputAmountBasedOnOutput — numerator is `(inputReserves * outputAmount) * 10000` — if inputReserves and outputAmount large, can overflow uint256 - TSwapPool::_swap — swap_count increment has no overflow protection (though uint256 is huge) — in unchecked block this could wrap to zero - TSwapPool::getPoolTokensToDepositBasedOnWeth — division by wethReserves; if wethReserves is zero (edge case) — division by zero reverts

TOKEN ACCOUNTING — - TSwapPool::_swap — transferFrom input AFTER computing output based on current balance — actual received amount could differ (fee-on-transfer token), breaking invariant - TSwapPool::swapExactInput — reads inputReserves = balanceOf(this) before transferFrom — if token is fee-on-transfer, actual received < inputAmount, outputAmount calculation wrong - TSwapPool::swapExactOutput — same issue; calculates inputAmount from reserves, then transferFrom that amount — if fee-on-transfer, reserves don't increase by inputAmount - TSwapPool::_addLiquidityMintAndTransfer — safeTransferFrom poolToken and weth; if either is fee-on-transfer — contract receives less than expected, breaks liquidity math - TSwapPool::withdraw — transfers out based on totalSupply and balanceOf; if previous fee-on-transfer deposits — reserves lower than expected, user may get less or tx reverts - TSwapPool::_swap — emits event with inputAmount/outputAmount; if fee-on-transfer — event is inaccurate, offchain indexers see wrong amounts - TSwapPool::sellPoolTokens — swapExactOutput with poolToken as input; wrong parameter order (poolTokenAmount is outputAmount, not inputAmount) — user pays wrong amount - TSwapPool::_swap — every 10 swaps transfers 1e18 of outputToken as bonus — if outputToken has 6 decimals (USDC), this is 1e12 tokens, pool drained instantly - TSwapPool::_swap — bonus transfer happens before main transfer; if outputReserves < 1e18 + outputAmount — main transfer will revert, swap fails

ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - TSwapPool::deposit — no check that wethToDeposit or maximumPoolTokensToDeposit is reasonable — attacker could pass max uint256, cause overflow in math - TSwapPool::deposit — no upper bound on liquidityTokensToMint — could mint more LP tokens than totalSupply can handle (unlikely but no cap) - TSwapPool::withdraw — no check liquidityTokensToBurn <= balanceOf(msg.sender) before math — if user passes huge number, _burn will revert but after expensive math - TSwapPool::swapExactInput — minOutputAmount is user-supplied; no cap — user could pass 0, accepting infinite slippage - TSwapPool::swapExactOutput — no maxInputAmount parameter — user has no slippage protection, could pay arbitrary input for fixed output - TSwapPool::getOutputAmountBasedOnInput — inputReserves not validated; if 0 — denominator could be just inputAmountMinusFee, wrong output - TSwapPool::getInputAmountBasedOnOutput — outputReserves - outputAmount in denominator; if outputAmount >= outputReserves — underflow (reverts) or wrong result - PoolFactory::createPool — tokenAddress not validated for zero or code existence — could create pool with EOA or nonexistent token - TSwapPool::deposit — deadline parameter exists in modifier but not used in function signature — function callable without deadline, modifier never checks

EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY — - TSwapPool::_addLiquidityMintAndTransfer — mints LP tokens then transfers tokens in — reentrancy on ERC777 poolToken callback could call withdraw, drain pool - TSwapPool::withdraw — burns LP tokens, emits event, then transfers both tokens — reentrancy on weth/poolToken transfer could call deposit, manipulate ratio - TSwapPool::_swap — emits Swap event with outputAmount, then transfers outputToken — reentrancy could call swapExactInput again, drain more than calculated - TSwapPool::_swap — transferFrom input, then transfer output; state not updated between — reentrancy on outputToken transfer could re-enter and swap again with stale reserves - TSwapPool::swapExactInput — reads reserves via balanceOf, computes output, calls _swap — if _swap re-enters via callback, balanceOf differs, invariant breaks - TSwapPool::_swap — bonus 1e18 token transfer before main transfer — if outputToken has callback, re-enter and get double bonus - TSwapPool::sellPoolTokens — external function calls swapExactOutput with block.timestamp — if called in fallback, timestamp stale, could bypass deadline check - TSwapPool::deposit — calls _addLiquidityMintAndTransfer which does external calls — if poolToken has malicious code, could re-enter deposit, double-mint LP

ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - TSwapPool::_swap — every 10th swap gives 1e18 bonus tokens — attacker can do 9 swaps of 1 wei, then 10th swap gets 1 ETH worth of tokens for free - TSwapPool::_swap — swap_count is global; increments on every swap (both directions) — user can front-run others to claim 10th swap bonus - TSwapPool::_swap — bonus uses fixed 1e18; if outputToken is WETH (18 decimals) bonus is 1 WETH, if poolToken is USDC (6 decimals) bonus is 1e12 USDC — incentive massively skewed - TSwapPool::deposit — initial deposit sets liquidity = wethToDeposit; subsequent deposits use ratio — first depositor can grief by depositing 1 wei weth + 1e18 poolToken, making all future deposits require huge poolToken amounts - TSwapPool::withdraw — rounds down on both token withdrawals — protocol slowly accumulates dust from LPs, invariant drifts - TSwapPool::getOutputAmountBasedOnInput — applies 0.3% fee via 997/1000 multiplier — but fee stays in reserves, inflating k, breaking stated invariant x*y=k - TSwapPool::getInputAmountBasedOnOutput — uses 997/10000 (should be 997/1000), applies 1% fee instead of 0.3% — users overpay 3x on exact-output swaps - TSwapPool::deposit — if totalSupply > 0, poolTokensToDeposit derived from wethToDeposit; user must approve max but only maxPoolTokensToDeposit checked — if ratio drifts (from fees), user could be forced to deposit more poolToken than approved, tx reverts - TSwapPool::_swap — no deadline check in _swap itself; only in public functions — if called via sellPoolTokens, uses block.timestamp as deadline, always passes, user has no expiry protection - TSwapPool::sellPoolTokens — wrong parameters to swapExactOutput: poolTokenAmount passed as outputAmount (WETH), but user wants to SELL poolToken — user receives poolTokenAmount WETH by paying calculated inputAmount poolToken, which is inverse of intent - TSwapPool::_addLiquidityMintAndTransfer — event emits (poolTokensToDeposit, wethToDeposit) but event definition is (wethDeposited, poolTokensDeposited) — offchain indexers see reversed amounts, breaks analytics - TSwapPool::swapExactOutput — no minInputAmount or maxInputAmount parameter — user blindly approves, could pay 10x expected if pool manipulated - TSwapPool::deposit — minimumLiquidityTokensToMint protects LP, but maximumPoolTokensToDeposit is only check on poolToken side — if poolToken price dumps between tx submission and execution, user forced to deposit devalued tokens - TSwapPool::getOutputAmountBasedOnInput — fee taken on input side (inputAmount * 997) — but docs say 0.3% stays in pool; actually reduces output, same effect but different from Uniswap v2 which adds fee to reserves explicitly - TSwapPool::getPriceOfOneWethInPoolTokens — uses 1e18 as input (assumes 18 decimals) — if poolToken has 6 decimals, price calculation is off by 1e12 - TSwapPool::deposit — MINIMUM_WETH_LIQUIDITY is 1e9 (1 gwei) — absurdly low, allows dust pools that are vulnerable to manipulation - TSwapPool::withdraw — user burns LP tokens to get pro-rata share; if pool has accumulated fees, ratio improved — later withdrawers get more value per LP than early withdrawers (not a bug per se, but fairness issue if not understood)
