ARRAY / INDEX / INPUT VALIDATION — - [ARRAY] Recovery::queuedPayloadHashes — unbounded array growth per wallet/signer — could cause DoS via gas exhaustion - [ARRAY] SessionSig::recoverSignature — attestationIndex read from flag without length check against attestationList — IndexOutOfBounds revert but after processing - [ARRAY] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — payload.calls.length == 0 rejected but sessionUsageLimits array resized without checking actualSize validity - [ARRAY] PermissionValidator::validatePermission — newUsageLimits array sized to usageLimits.length + rules.length but actualLimitsCount may exceed — assembly resize could mask issues - [INPUT VALIDATION] Recovery::queuePayload — _payload not validated for noChainId consistency — could queue payload that never executes - [INPUT VALIDATION] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — payload.space <= MAX_SPACE but no check that space is allocated/valid for wallet - [INPUT VALIDATION] Passkeys::_decodeSignature — flags parsing trusts bytesAuthDataSize, bytesClientDataJSONSize etc. without sanity bounds — malformed signature could cause slice OOB - [INPUT VALIDATION] SessionSig::_decodePermissions — length read as uint8 but permissions array allocated without checking encoded has enough bytes

6. EXTERNAL CALLS & REENTRANCY — - [REENTRANCY] ImplicitSessionManager::_validateImplicitCall — external call to ISignalsImplicitMode(call.to).acceptImplicitRequest before validation complete — untrusted contract could reenter - [REENTRANCY] Recovery::isValidSignature — external call to IERC1271(_signer).isValidSignature with no reentrancy guard — malicious signer contract could reenter queuePayload - [EXTERNAL CALLS] Passkeys::recoverSapientSignatureCompact — calls WebAuthn.verify which may have side effects — no validation of WebAuthn library behavior

7. CROSS-CHAIN / BRIDGE — - [CROSS-CHAIN] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — sessionPermissions.chainId == 0 means any chain but no replay protection across chains with same wallet address - [CROSS-CHAIN] Recovery::recoveryPayloadHash — _payload.noChainId flag allows cross-chain replay if queued on multiple chains - [CROSS-CHAIN] SessionSig::recoverConfiguration — chainId validation in explicit sessions but implicit sessions have no chain restrictions

8. ECONOMIC / INCENTIVE LOGIC — - [ECONOMIC] Recovery::queuePayload — no cost to queue, allows griefing by filling storage with fake recovery attempts - [ECONOMIC] ExplicitSessionManager::_validateLimitUsageIncrement — requires incrementUsageLimit as first call but no gas refund if limits unused — user pays for no-op - [ECONOMIC] PermissionValidator::validatePermission — cumulative limits stored per wallet but no mechanism to reset or decrease — stuck limits could brick sessions - [ECONOMIC] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — sessionUsageLimits.totalValueUsed compared to valueLimit but no slippage protection if value changes during execution - [ECONOMIC] ImplicitSessionManager::_validateImplicitCall — blacklist check is O(log n) binary search but insertion/removal not defined — stale blacklist could allow banned signers - [INCENTIVE] Recovery::timestampForQueuedPayload — queued timestamp never expires, allows indefinite queueing without cleanup incentive - [INCENTIVE] ExplicitSessionManager::incrementUsageLimit — can only increment usage, never decrement — session becomes unusable if limit incorrectly incremented ---

Additional Concerns — - [ARITHMETIC] LibBytes::readUintX — dynamic size reads (1-32 bytes) without validation that pointer + sizeSize < encoded.length — could read garbage - [ARRAY] SessionSig::recoverConfiguration — maxPermissionsSize guess could allocate massive array if encoded.length is large — DoS via memory exhaustion - [INPUT VALIDATION] Recovery::_recoverBranch — FLAG_NODE reads 32 bytes but no validation node is non-zero — could inject zero nodes into tree - [ARITHMETIC] SessionSig::recoverSignature — pointer += dataSize without checking dataSize < encodedSignature.length - pointer — slice could be empty or overlapping - [ECONOMIC] Recovery::queuePayload — emit NewQueuedPayload before checking signature validity (wait, no, signature IS checked first, but event spam possible on valid signatures) - [ARRAY] SessionManager::recoverSapientSignature — actualSessionUsageLimits resized via assembly mstore without zeroing unused slots — could leak stale data - [INPUT VALIDATION] Passkeys::_decodeSignature — fallback abi.decode path (flags & 0x20) trusts encoded data structure — malicious input could cause decode revert or memory corruption - [REENTRANCY] PermissionValidator::setLimitUsage — emits event that external observers could use to reenter via callback pattern - [ECONOMIC] SessionManager::MAX_SPACE — restricts space to uint80 max but no migration path if nonce space exhausted - [ARITHMETIC] ImplicitSessionManager::_isAddressBlacklisted — binary search int256 mid calculation could overflow on extremely large arrays - [INPUT VALIDATION] LibAttestation::fromPacked — applicationData size is uint24 but no check that newPointer + dataSize <= encoded.length ---
